wrong_choice_explanations: 終わり means 'finish/end' — it expresses completion rather than the beginning of rain.
続け means 'continue' — it indicates ongoing action rather than the start of rain.
やめ means 'stop/quit' — it expresses cessation rather than the beginning of rain.
込み means 'into/thoroughly' — it indicates intensity or depth of action, not the beginning of singing.
上げ means 'raise up' — it indicates upward movement or completion, not starting to sing.
返し means 'return/give back' — it indicates returning something, not beginning to sing.
止み means 'stop/cease' — it indicates ending rather than beginning to cry.
直り means 'be fixed/get better' — it indicates improvement, not starting to cry.
切り means 'cut/disconnect' — it indicates cutting or ending something, not starting to cry.

exercises.example_sentence: 学生たちが話し__始めました。
wrong_choice_explanations: 過ぎ means 'too much/excessively' — it expresses excess rather than the beginning of talking.
かけ means 'about to do/halfway through' — it indicates an incomplete state, not the start of talking.
合い means 'together/mutually' — while students talking together makes sense, it doesn't express the beginning like 出し does.

exercises.example_sentence: 花が咲き__始めています。
exercises.translation: The flowers are starting to bloom.
wrong_choice_explanations: 終わり means 'finish/end' — it indicates completion of blooming rather than the start.
散り means 'scatter/fall' — it indicates petals falling, not flowers beginning to bloom.
枯れ means 'wither/die' — it indicates plants dying, not starting to bloom.
回り means 'turn/go around' — it indicates circular movement, not starting to run.
止まり means 'stop/halt' — it indicates stopping rather than beginning to run.
疲れ means 'get tired' — it indicates fatigue, not starting to run.

exercises.example_sentence: 時計が動き__始めました。
wrong_choice_explanations: 止まり means 'stop' — it indicates the clock stopping rather than starting to work.
壊れ means 'break down' — it indicates the clock breaking, not starting to function.
遅れ means 'be late/slow' — it indicates the clock running slow, not beginning to work.
止み means 'stop/cease' — it indicates the wind stopping rather than starting to blow.
強まり means 'get stronger' — it indicates wind intensifying, not starting to blow.
弱まり means 'get weaker' — it indicates wind weakening, not beginning to blow.
止み means 'stop/cease' — it indicates the phone stopping rather than starting to ring.
切れ means 'be cut off/disconnected' — it indicates the phone line being cut, not starting to ring.
壊れ means 'break down' — it indicates the phone breaking, not beginning to ring.

exercises.example_sentence: 子供が笑い__始めました。
wrong_choice_explanations: 泣き means 'cry' — it indicates crying rather than starting to laugh.
止み means 'stop/cease' — it indicates stopping rather than beginning to laugh.
疲れ means 'get tired' — it indicates fatigue, not starting to laugh.
